Venue Guides 12 min read

Best Annapolis Wedding Venues: Waterfront and Historic

Annapolis wedding venues with honest pricing: waterfront parks, historic mansions, and Chesapeake Bay settings. A Maryland planner's real guide for 2026.

J

John

Baltimore Wedding Planner

Featured image, replace with real photo

Annapolis is one of Maryland's strongest wedding destinations. The Chesapeake Bay waterfront, centuries-old colonial buildings, and a walkable downtown create a setting that's genuinely hard to replicate anywhere else in the state. The tradeoff is cost. Annapolis venues run 15 to 25% higher than comparable Baltimore spaces, and the best waterfront locations book 12 to 18 months in advance.

I've coordinated weddings and elopements at venues across Annapolis and I know which ones deliver on their photos and which ones don't. Here's what actually matters when you're comparing your options, with pricing that reflects what couples are actually paying in 2026.

What Are the Best Waterfront Wedding Venues in Annapolis?

Annapolis's waterfront venues are the reason most couples choose the city. Chesapeake Bay views, sailboat-lined docks, and open water backdrops create a ceremony aesthetic that's distinctive and genuinely beautiful. Here are the best options.

Quiet Waters Park

Waterfront / Park Up to 300 guests $500 to $2,000 permit

A 340-acre Anne Arundel County park on the South River, about 2 miles from downtown Annapolis. The David A. Winston Garden offers formal garden ceremony space with river views in the background. Indoor pavilions handle receptions up to 300. This is the best value waterfront venue in the Annapolis area. Permit and pavilion rental fees run $500 to $2,000 depending on the space and day. Outside catering is allowed through the park's approved vendor list. It's the venue I recommend most often for elopements and micro-weddings in the Annapolis area, and it works well for larger weddings too. Book 6 to 12 months out for peak season dates.

Annapolis Maritime Museum (East Campus)

Waterfront / Museum Up to 150 guests $3,000 to $6,000

Located on Back Creek in Eastport, directly across from the Annapolis City Dock. The waterfront lawn holds outdoor ceremonies with views of the marina and Chesapeake Bay. The indoor museum space is available for receptions when weather requires it. Outside catering is permitted through approved vendors. Capacity is around 150 guests, which makes it a natural fit for mid-size weddings. Venue fees run $3,000 to $6,000. It's a genuine waterfront location, not a water-adjacent one, which matters a lot in photos.

Annapolis City Dock Area

Public Waterfront Small groups (under 25) No permit for small groups

The public waterfront around City Dock, the Naval Academy seawall, and the Eastport waterfront park are all public spaces where small ceremonies (under 25 people) don't require permits. For elopements and intimate ceremonies, the sailboat-filled harbor creates one of the most immediately recognizable Maryland wedding backdrops. You'll need to bring your own officiant and any ceremony elements, and you should time it for early morning or weekday to avoid tourist crowds. Larger groups need Anne Arundel County or City of Annapolis event permits.

What Are the Best Historic Wedding Venues in Annapolis?

Annapolis has more pre-Revolutionary War buildings per square block than almost any city in the country. For couples who want genuine colonial-era architecture rather than a venue that's been designed to look historic, Annapolis delivers it.

William Paca House and Garden

Historic / Garden Up to 100 guests $2,500 to $5,000

A preserved 18th-century colonial mansion with a five-part formal garden that's been authentically restored. William Paca was a signer of the Declaration of Independence, so you're getting married on genuine American history. The tiered garden with boxwood hedges, a fish pond, and original colonial plantings is unlike any other ceremony setting in Maryland. Capacity is limited to around 100, which keeps the space intimate. Fees run $2,500 to $5,000. Outside catering is permitted through approved vendors. This is one of the most distinctive venues in Maryland for couples who care about authentic historic character.

Historic Inns of Annapolis

Historic Hotel Up to 200 guests $5,000 to $12,000

Three 18th-century buildings in the heart of downtown Annapolis, steps from the State House and Maryland's oldest streets. The Governor Calvert House ballroom is the primary event space and seats up to 200 for dinner. In-house catering is available through their culinary team. The location puts your guests in the middle of colonial Annapolis, which is genuinely hard to replicate. Venue fees run $5,000 to $12,000. It's on the higher end for the Annapolis market, but the combination of historic architecture and central location justifies it for couples who've decided Annapolis is where they're getting married.

Maryland Hall for the Creative Arts

Arts / Historic Up to 200 guests $3,000 to $7,000

A restored 1930s art deco building in Annapolis's arts district with theater spaces, gallery rooms, and a historic courtyard. It's a less obvious choice than the waterfront venues, but it's genuinely distinctive and works well for couples who want Annapolis proximity without the waterfront premium. Outside catering is permitted. Venue fees run $3,000 to $7,000. A good option for couples who want an Annapolis venue that doesn't look like every other Annapolis wedding photo.

What Are the Best Annapolis Wedding Venues for Larger Receptions?

Most Annapolis historic and waterfront venues cap out around 150 to 200 guests. If you're planning a larger reception, the hotel ballrooms are the primary option.

Westin Annapolis

Hotel Ballroom Up to 350 guests $8,000 to $15,000+

The largest dedicated wedding venue in the Annapolis market with multiple ballroom configurations and full hotel guest room blocks for out-of-town guests. In-house catering is required. Total wedding costs at the Westin typically run higher than other Annapolis venues because of food and beverage minimums on top of the venue fee. It's the right choice if you need 250+ guests, want in-house catering management, and your budget can support it. Venue fees run $8,000 to $15,000+ before catering minimums.

Loews Annapolis Hotel

Hotel / Downtown Up to 300 guests $7,000 to $14,000+

Located in downtown Annapolis near the State House, the Loews offers ballroom and courtyard event spaces in a central location. In-house catering is required. The downtown location means guests can walk to Annapolis's restaurants and shops after the reception. Slightly lower pricing than the Westin for comparable capacity, though still on the higher end of the Annapolis market before catering minimums.

How Much Do Annapolis Wedding Venues Cost Compared to Baltimore?

Annapolis venues cost 15 to 25% more than comparable Baltimore spaces for similar quality and guest capacity. Here's how the tiers compare.

Tier Annapolis Baltimore What you get for the premium
Budget / Park$500 to $2,000$50 to $1,800Chesapeake Bay waterfront vs inland park
Mid-Range$3,000 to $7,000$2,000 to $6,000Sailboat harbor vs industrial or garden
Premium$7,000 to $15,000$5,000 to $12,000Colonial downtown setting vs city venue

If the Annapolis setting is what you're looking for, the premium's usually worth it. If it isn't what's driving your venue search, Baltimore's got more variety at lower price points. I've planned weddings in both cities and the question I always ask is: when you picture your wedding photos, is Annapolis specifically what you're seeing? If the answer's yes, book Annapolis. If it's not, don't pay the premium.

What Should You Know Before Booking an Annapolis Wedding Venue?

Annapolis has some specific logistical considerations that don't apply to most other Maryland markets.

  • Parking is genuinely difficult. Downtown Annapolis has limited parking. If you're booking a downtown venue, you'll need a parking plan for guests. Most hotels have garages, but park venues require shuttle coordination from remote lots for larger groups.
  • Naval Academy graduation weekend fills the city. Late May graduation weekend brings 50,000+ visitors to Annapolis. If you're considering a late May date, confirm hotel availability for guests well in advance.
  • Peak season runs longer than Baltimore. The sailing season and summer tourism means Annapolis venues stay busy from April through October. Weekend dates book faster than comparable Baltimore venues.
  • Noise curfews vary by location. Waterfront and park venues often require music to stop by 10pm. Confirm the curfew before booking if you're planning a late reception.

Planning an Annapolis wedding?

John has coordinated events at Quiet Waters Park, the Maritime Museum, William Paca House, and several downtown venues. He knows which ones have parking logistics figured out and which ones still need work.

Annapolis Wedding Planner

Frequently Asked Questions About Annapolis Wedding Venues

What's the most affordable wedding venue in Annapolis?

Quiet Waters Park is the best value in the Annapolis market, with permits and pavilion rental starting around $500 and going up to $2,000 for full use of the main pavilion and garden areas. It's a genuine waterfront setting with South River views. For smaller ceremonies, Annapolis City Dock and the surrounding public waterfront areas don't require permits for groups under 25 people.

Which Annapolis venues allow outside catering?

Quiet Waters Park, Annapolis Maritime Museum, William Paca House, and Maryland Hall all allow outside catering through approved vendor lists. The hotel venues (Westin, Loews) require in-house catering. Always ask about the catering policy before touring, since it's the biggest variable in total venue cost.

How far in advance should you book an Annapolis wedding venue?

For popular Annapolis venues (Quiet Waters, Historic Inns, Maritime Museum), peak season Saturdays from May through October book 12 to 18 months in advance. Weekday and off-season dates are available with much shorter lead times. The Westin and Loews have more capacity and sometimes have Saturday availability with 6 to 9 months notice, though not reliably.

Can you elope in Annapolis?

Yes. Annapolis is one of Maryland's best elopement locations. Quiet Waters Park, City Dock, the Annapolis seawall, and William Paca Garden are all accessible options. Small groups under 25 don't need permits at most public locations. You'll need a Maryland marriage license (available at Anne Arundel County Circuit Court in Annapolis), a registered officiant, and one witness. See Maryland elopement packages.

John has coordinated elopements and full weddings in Annapolis. If you're considering a venue not listed here, he can give you an honest assessment of whether it's a fit for your budget and guest count.
